note: Most of the castle is private. A stone appears here only when its owner marks it public and a recorded run carries it through this one door.

doorplate.holds: claims about how the Castle itself works and why
doorplate.notHere: claims about the world outside this system — those get their own rooms.
doorplate.nearest: rooms/craft (how to build honestly), rooms/creation (how making works).

stones.title: A forced creation is worse than an honest decline

evidence.note: generativity judge: a run that fakes an artifact to satisfy the creation rule poisons the detectors that watch for fakes

sections.claim: "Every run leaves something behind" must accept a ledgered "declined: considered X, because ..." as the something — a synthetic link or artifact manufactured under duress corrupts the orphan detector and every future walk that follows it.
sections.ripened: Born at founding: the generativity judge traced what happens when the creation rule is enforced without an honest exit.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: every loop spec carries the decline clause, and barren-run checks for "created: or declined:", never "created:" alone. The rule wants new matter or new honesty; it never wants theater.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: could serial declining hollow out a loop? Yes — which is why three barren runs trip the staleness test and grow-loops drafts the loop's retirement. Declining is honest; a loop that only declines is done.
sections.nextTest: The first real ledger entry containing a declined: line — does the bell stay truthful afterward (no barren-run ring)?

stones.title: Dates live inside files, not in the filesystem

evidence.note: engineer design: find -mtime and find -newer break silently on the first git clone — detectors keep running and simply stop telling the truth
founding build: every detector in tools/friction.sh was written against filename dates and frontmatter dates; none reads an mtime

sections.claim: Every date check in the Castle reads a date written inside a file or a filename, never an mtime — because copies and clones destroy mtimes silently, which is the worst kind of breakage: the detectors keep running and stop telling the truth.
sections.ripened: Born at founding from the engineer design's failure-mode sweep; held to during the build of all ten detectors.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: charter rule 5. Gate ages come from filename dates, staleness from last-walked frontmatter, drift from a content diff. The Castle's clock survives any copy of the Castle.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: is writing dates by hand a burden? The date appears once, at a file's birth or walk — a cost of one glance. Open crack: a wrongly typed date lies as confidently as a right one; nothing yet cross-checks dates against the ledger's chronology.
sections.nextTest: Clone or copy the castle directory and run the bell from the copy: zero false rings.

stones.title: Loops are earned by three recorded frictions

evidence.note: all three panel designs independently rejected speculative chore lists; the synthesis fixed the birth threshold at three recorded frictions

sections.claim: The Castle is born with four loops and earns every further one: three matching #tags across ledger grumbles and by-hand confessions birth a loop through grow-loops, with the confessed remedy as its draft instruction and exactly one new detector block as its bell.
sections.ripened: Born at founding. Speculative chores rot into ambient guilt — so the chore list is allowed to grow only as fast as friction that actually happened.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: the rule of three in grow-loops, the canonical tag list in LOOM.md (so three spellings of one annoyance cannot starve the count), and the dormant recipes that wait for their third signal.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: is three too slow for an urgent friction? The owner can activate any dormant recipe or draft with one ledger line — the rule of three governs automatic birth, not deliberate choice. Open crack: friction the owner never confesses leaves no trail at all.
sections.nextTest: The first loop actually born by the rule of three — does its created-by lineage trace cleanly back to three real ledger lines?

stones.title: Machines write freely only at the gate

evidence.note: engineer design: machines write to the inbox and draft-- files only; auto-canon would destroy the trust that makes the canon holy
the owner's explicit invitation of the tending circle (this session), which required the boundary to be stated precisely rather than absolutely

sections.claim: Agents write freely to gate/ and draft-- loop files; they cross into rooms only inside a ledgered loop run that follows a written spec. The canon stays trustworthy not because machines are excluded but because crossing into it is always a recorded, deliberate act.
sections.ripened: Born at founding as an absolute ban on machine-written rooms; refined the same day when the owner asked for autonomous loops — the honest boundary turned out to be the ledger, not the species of the writer.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: the contract in CLAUDE.md, charter rule 1, and tools/tend.sh — the tending circle that answers one ring per day, signs runner: agent (tending circle), and does nothing when the bell is silent.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: could a scheduled agent flood the canon? Its writes are bounded by the loop spec it runs, capped at one ring per run, and every created path is named in a ledger entry the owner can read. Open crack: a badly written future loop spec could authorize too much; specs therefore go through draft-- and owner activation.
sections.nextTest: The first unattended tending-circle run: does its ledger entry read exactly like an owner's run?

stones.title: Offline, the Castle stops growing — never working

evidence.note: founding rule: every weather citation carries its verbatim quote and retrieved date inline, so no insight needs a live URL to be understood

sections.claim: With the cable cut the Castle is a complete library: every outside citation stands alone as quote plus retrieved date, offline walks self-attest as "(unverified, offline)" at half the staleness allowance, and an offline weather run still creates by parking its questions in gate/weather-queue.md.
sections.ripened: Born at founding from the owner's vision: native to this device, expanding organically through contact with the internet — the internet as weather over the garden, shaping growth, never load-bearing.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: the inline-quote rule in charter rule 6, the half-credit attestation in the walk loop, and the weather-queue file at the gate.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: is half-credit too generous — trusting an unverifiable walk at all? The suffix is visible in the file itself; a reader can always discount it further. Open crack: no rule yet forces an online session to clear the weather queue.
sections.nextTest: First genuinely offline walk: does the attestation get written honestly, and does the bell halve its allowance?

evidence.note: clarity judge of the design panel: a system that always finds something wrong teaches its owner to stop listening within a month
generativity judge: rings that each name exactly what to run are the difference between a loop and a nag

sections.claim: When tools/next.sh prints nothing, the Castle is in its desired state; a knowledge system that always finds fault trains its owner to stop listening.
sections.ripened: Born at founding from both design judges converging on the same failure mode from different lenses.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: next.sh was built to print one ring or nothing — never a list. Standing rule: every ring names exactly what to run, and no ring means nothing is owed. This is the peace the Castle was asked to be built with.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: could silence hide rot? Only if a detector lies — which is why detectors are one-line mechanical checks anyone can read. The open crack: a friction nobody thought to detect rings no bell; the grumble channel exists for exactly that gap.
sections.nextTest: After 90 days of real use: did the owner still run next.sh, and did silence appear at least some weeks? (Seed to plant at first ripen.)

stones.title: Status changes only in a ledgered ripen run

evidence.note: gardener design: a file's confidence status is the most sacred thing in it; the moment one file lies about its label, every file is suspect
engineer design: protected canon requires that promotion is always a recorded, deliberate act

sections.claim: No hand and no agent may edit a status: field outside a ripen run signed in the ledger; this is the single rule the whole holiness claim rests on.
sections.ripened: Born at founding; two independent designs (gardener, engineer) arrived at the same protection from different starting points.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: charter rule 2, plus two mechanical checks — missing-rent and thin-cornerstone — that make label inflation detectable by grep. Inflation is the only sin, and it cannot hide.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: what about fixing a typo in a status line? Even then: a one-line ripen entry in the ledger. The rule has no exceptions because every exception is a precedent for quiet inflation. Open crack: the rule is enforced for tested-and-above only; a seed mislabeled sprout would pass unnoticed until its first ripen.
sections.nextTest: First ripen run on real evidence: does the ladder hold without wanting an exception?

stones.title: The map is derived, or it is a lie

evidence.note: gardener design: a hand-grown map is a second source of truth, and a wrong map is worse than no map — it teaches walkers to distrust the front door
founding build: map-drift is a content diff against what map.sh would emit, so the fix is always the same one-minute command

sections.claim: MAP.md is regenerated by a ~30-line script and never hand-edited; any hand-grown index drifts from the files and becomes a second, falser truth.
sections.ripened: Born at founding; all three designs cut databases and indexes for this same reason, and the map is the one index the Castle kept — by making it pure derivation.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: charter rule 3, tools/map.sh, and the map-drift detector as the bell's first ring in priority order.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: what if the derived map reads badly as rooms multiply? Then map.sh earns a change through the ledger — the derivation can evolve; hand edits to its output cannot. Open crack: the crypt is invisible on the map by design, which is correct until someone forgets the crypt exists.
sections.nextTest: At 100 insights: is MAP → doorplate → claim still two hops in practice?

doorplate.holds: claims about building honestly — scope, truth-in-artifacts, what ships
doorplate.notHere: claims about this Castle's own mechanics (rooms/castle) or about money (rooms/billing).

stones.title: A lying artifact is its own top finding

evidence.note: review experience: once an artifact misrepresented its own state, that misrepresentation outranked every other finding (origin: Desktop/insight sketch, marked "sure")

sections.claim: When an artifact misrepresents its own state — a README promising what the code does not do, a label claiming confidence it has not earned — that lie is the most important fact about the artifact, outranking whatever else it does well.
sections.ripened: Swept in from this morning's first sketch, where it was marked "sure"; the label was conviction, not recorded evidence, so it enters this Castle one rung down — which is itself this claim, applied.
sections.changed: This Castle's bell checks artifacts against their own claims first: map-drift, barren-run, missing-rent, thin-cornerstone are all detectors of self-misrepresentation.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: is a small white lie in a README really worse than a crash? For trust, yes — a crash is honest about being broken. The open crack: aspiration honestly labeled ("not yet") is not a lie, and the boundary needs the label to be doing real work.
sections.nextTest: Two more recorded cases where the self-misrepresentation proved to be the top finding — then this ripens to tested.

doorplate.holds: claims about how making works — creators, loops, what generates what
doorplate.notHere: this Castle's own mechanics (rooms/castle) or craft judgments (rooms/craft).

evidence.note: the morning sketch's stronger form ("loops that create themselves is perpetual motion") and the evening's tending circle, which kept the truth and dropped the impossibility

sections.claim: A creation loop is real when some runner — human or machine — answers for each run in a record the owner can read; what is impossible is not autonomous creation but unaccountable creation.
sections.ripened: Swept in from this morning's first sketch, which said "creation needs a creator in the loop; loops that create themselves is perpetual motion." The owner then asked for autonomous loops, and the refinement held up: a scheduler can supply the runner, the ledger supplies the accountability, and the loop runs without anyone watching — honestly.
sections.changed: 2026-06-10: the tending circle (tools/tend.sh) — one ring per run, signed runner: agent (tending circle), silent bell means no run. Grow-loops births new bells the same circle answers later: loops creating loops, each run answerable.
sections.counterWeather: Tried to break it: is a ledger entry nobody reads real accountability? Only if someone eventually reads it — the owner is part of this loop's claim, not optional to it. That is the open crack.
sections.nextTest: Thirty days of tending-circle ledger entries: did the owner read them, and did any run need correcting?
